name: AKK-WST01 metabolic response depended on baseline Akkermansia abundance
summary: The AKK-WST01 trial supports microbiota-guided human biomarker relevance while weakening any one-size-fits-all live-biotherapeutic claim.
statement: In a 12-week randomized trial in adults with overweight or obese type 2 diabetes, AKK-WST01 did not produce significant overall between-group improvements in body weight or HbA1c, but participants with low baseline Akkermansia showed better colonization and reductions in weight, fat mass, and HbA1c.
population_or_model: Adults with overweight or obese type 2 diabetes
quantitative_note: The abstract reports no significant overall between-group differences for body weight and HbA1c, with positive subgroup effects in participants with low baseline Akkermansia.
caveats: The clinical context is type 2 diabetes and obesity rather than healthy aging.
The result argues for microbiota-guided selection rather than broad use of the same live biotherapeutic.
The ClinicalTrials.gov record status was listed as unknown when checked on 2026-06-01, although the publication reports results.
